category: sold-out product with a stale price still displayed

consistency.notes: All non-null, non-empty ground-truth labels are supported by the page text. 'coffee.roaster_country' is not stated verbatim, but it is reasonably entailed by 'Salem, Massachusetts' as United States. 'coffee.is_blend' is supported by the single-origin presentation ('Origin: Kenya' with named producer/factory/varieties) and no indication of blending. 'coffee.is_espresso' is supported by 'Roast: Light / Filter' and the product being presented as filter coffee rather than espresso. 'price.price_type' is supported by the standard listed product price with no subscription/recurring pricing indicated on the product page.

realism.reasons: Very plausible as an html-to-text dump of a real specialty roaster product page. The structure is highly typical: nav/header noise, product title, sold-out state, price, bag size, notify/add-to-cart language, concise green-coffee specs, tasting notes, short origin story, roast/use positioning, brew suggestion, related products, variant block, and footer. The coffee details are coherent and industry-realistic: Kenya from Nyeri/Mathira, factory and cooperative naming, washed process, common Kenyan varieties (SL28, SL34, Ruiru 11, Batian), plausible altitude, AA separation, and a harvest window that fits Kenya’s main crop timing. Tasting notes and descriptive copy read like normal specialty coffee marketing without obvious AI awkwardness. The variant data is also believable: 250g and 1kg options, sensible SKUs, sold-out inventory, and pricing that fits the market. Minor tiny tells only: the explicit sentence explaining the displayed price is ‘for reference only’ feels slightly platform-generated, and ‘default_for_inference’ in the variant block looks like an extraction artifact rather than site copy—but that supports the idea of a rendered/exported page rather than undermining realism.

label.coffee.producer_text: Gichathaini Factory sits near the town of Karatina in Nyeri and is one of the washing stations operated by the Gikanda Farmers Cooperative Society. Smallholder members deliver ripe cherry during the main harvest, and the factory team sorts, depulps, ferments, washes, and slow-dries the parchment on raised tables.

coffee.producer_text: Gichathaini Factory sits near the town of Karatina in Nyeri and is one of the washing stations operated by the Gikanda Farmers Cooperative Society. Smallholder members deliver ripe cherry during the main harvest, and the factory team sorts, depulps, ferments, washes, and slow-dries the parchment on raised tables. We bought this AA separation for its classic Nyeri structure: bright citrus acidity, deep blackcurrant sweetness, and a long, tea-like finish. It is roasted light for filter brewing, where we find the florals and syrupy red-fruit character are most expressive.

source_snippets.snippet: Tasting Notes: blackcurrant, grapefruit marmalade, demerara sugar, rosehip
